If at a query the big toe be touched or shaken, the querist

Brhat SamhitZ
would suffer from eye-disease. If a finger is touched, his daughter
will have grief. If he strikes his head, he will have trouble from the
king.
[The author's use of the accusative in is hardly
defensible, though the commentator supplies the verb For,
the author has already used the verb with which would
go better.

If the querist touches his chest, he will have separation from
a near and dear one ; if he takes off his cloth from his body, he will
undergo some calamity. Should he take a piece of cloth and join
his feet together he would attain his cherished object.
